title: Two-Band Weyl Model With A Tunable Pair
summary: The toy Hamiltonian with off-diagonal p1 plus or minus i p2 and diagonal plus or minus f(p3) realizes Weyl nodes whose creation, motion, and annihilation are controlled by the zeros of f.
assumptions: Zeros of f are simple unless one is studying node coalescence.
regime: Continuum toy model for pairs of Weyl nodes.
scope: Lecture One explicit example used to create or annihilate Weyl nodes.

source_anchors.notes: The parameter a in f(p3)=p3^2-a controls pair creation and annihilation.

representation: Explicit 2x2 toy Hamiltonian for a Weyl-node pair.

symbols.meaning: controls the location and multiplicity of Weyl points
tuning parameter in the example f(p3)=p3^2-a

failure_modes: This model is local and does not by itself enforce lattice periodicity.
retrieval_hints: Use when a query asks for an explicit Hamiltonian that creates Weyl-node pairs.
